title: AFT sidecar for laps-until-next-stop, then convert to next-lap hazard
summary: This target is naturally a time-to-event problem.
rationale: This target is naturally a time-to-event problem. XGBoost’s AFT objective explicitly supports censored time-to-event labels, which matches drivers who reach race end before another stop. A sidecar predicting remaining laps until the next pit can add a smoother notion of stop imminence than a pure binary next-lap model.
implementation_hint: Create per-driver/per-race labels for `laps_to_next_pit`; right-censor sequences with no future pit before race end. Train an XGBoost `survival:aft` model on current-lap state, then transform predicted remaining-time distributions into `P(stop in 1 lap)` or use the AFT score as a blend feature for the main classifier.
expected_effect: Better discrimination near the pit window and fewer brittle threshold effects, especially on long stints and end-of-race censored cases.
risk: AFT optimization target is not ROC AUC, so standalone performance may lag; the win may come mainly as a sidecar feature or blend member.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
